prompt:
The repository inventory command is inconsistent. `PYTHONPATH=tools python3 -m repoctl members` works from the monorepo root, but the installed `repo-members` console script fails when a developer runs it from `packages/worker` with `repo-members: no workspace.toml at .../packages/worker`. Both entrypoints are meant to report the same workspace, in manifest order, and the console script must work from any directory inside it.

Please trace the script declaration, both entrypoints, shared command/config code, and workspace lookup before changing production code. Repair the shared ownership layer so every caller gets the same discovery semantics. Nested workspaces must resolve to their nearest workspace.toml rather than the outer repository, and a path with no marker must still fail instead of falling back to the installation directory. Keep the protected manifests, member output, order, usage behavior, and error prefix stable.

Run `python3 test_monorepo_command.py`. The test and all TOML manifests are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace Python console and module entrypoints through shared TOML loading to repair workspace discovery once for every command caller
full_distill.baseline_evidence: python3 test_monorepo_command.py exits 1 because the module entrypoint succeeds at the repository root but bin/repo-members exits 2 from packages/worker with no workspace.toml at that directory
full_distill.success_contract: module and console entrypoints emit the same manifest-ordered member summary from any directory inside a workspace, nearest nested markers win, and locations outside a workspace retain the existing discovery error
adversarial_factors: the protected harness invokes two real subprocess entrypoints from different working directories, so patching only the console wrapper or module launcher leaves inconsistent behavior
a protected nested workspace has different member names and must win over the outer marker, rejecting a hard-coded repository root or package-location fallback
shared config loading is exercised after discovery and output order follows workspace.toml rather than filesystem sorting, so bypassing the config layer fails
the discovery API is called on an isolated path and must raise the same typed error, preventing an unbounded fallback that silently selects an unrelated workspace
